steps.title: Use the zero condition to relate a and b
steps.content:
Since $g(-9) = 0$ and $g(x) = \frac{f(x)}{x+3}$, the numerator must be zero: $f(-9) = 0$.
For $f(x) = ax + b$, this gives $-9a + b = 0$, so $b = 9a$.

steps.title: Find the coefficients and y-intercept
steps.content:
Use $x = -21, g(-21) = 2$:
$$2 = \frac{-21a + b}{-21 + 3} = \frac{-21a + b}{-18}$$
$$-36 = -21a + b$$

Substitute $b = 9a$:
$$-36 = -21a + 9a = -12a$$
$$a = 3$$

Therefore $b = 9(3) = 27$, so $f(x) = 3x + 27$.

The $y$-intercept is $(0, 27)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
